chief_complaint: A 63-year-old male with hypertension and diabetes mellitus presented with non-ST elevation myocardial infarction (NSTEMI).
history: Percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) of left main (LM) bifurcation lesions carries a high risk of complications, including stent entrapment. A 63-year-old male with hypertension and diabetes mellitus presented with NSTEMI. PCI was performed using the T and small protrusion (TAP) technique for a 1,1,1 Medina-classified an LM bifurcation lesion.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: ECG: non-ST elevation myocardial infarction (NSTEMI). Bloods: hypertension and diabetes mellitus. Imaging: PCI of left main (LM) bifurcation lesions.
correct_diagnosis: Stent entrapment in a previously stented left main coronary artery
differentials: Stent entrapment in a previously stented left main coronary artery
Myocardial infarction due to stent thrombosis
Cardiac arrest due to ventricular fibrillation
correct_ranking: Stent entrapment in a previously stented left main coronary artery
Myocardial infarction due to stent thrombosis
Cardiac arrest due to ventricular fibrillation
